target: ISS-2318 / SEC-part2b-stratification-paragraph.c1 hole 7 — soften the closing sentence of the §2b.1 relabelling-axis paragraph from causal-attribution ('avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging') to structural framing that defers the active-avoidance reading to the orthogonal evidence the surrounding paragraphs already supply (the relabels_standard=true pattern above; the SC-monitoring-hardcoded-to-pass pattern below).
kernel_of_truth: The dome defender's response to our 'avoids exactly the categories' framing has a real point: a monitor.py-style validator needs an API. Geomagnetic indices (NOAA Kp/Dst), eclipse circumstances (NASA Besselian elements), seismic catalogs (USGS), aurora oval positions (NOAA SWPC) all have queryable feeds — and that is where the dome's monitoring concentrates. Firmament thickness, sub-terrestrial toroidal currents, dome-edge curvature, and theological assertions have no public feed to plug into. A passive-availability explanation for the monitoring=none pattern in those categories is therefore on the table — the bare stratification count alone does not refute it. The active-avoidance reading is supplied (correctly) by the *other* axes the surrounding paragraphs document: the relabels_standard=true pattern (rebadging is an active editorial choice, not a passive consequence of missing APIs) and the SC-monitoring-hardcoded-to-pass pattern (validation code exists but is built to pass — a clearer active-avoidance signal than absence of code).
why_it_doesnt_save_claim_for_dome_defend: Softening the closing sentence to a structural observation does not weaken our overall argument because the active-avoidance reading is structurally supplied by the orthogonal evidence in the adjacent paragraphs, not by the stratification count itself. A defender saying 'we monitor what has APIs' has to also explain (a) why dome-versions of established physics get systematically RELABELLED (active editorial work, not API gaps) and (b) why the hardcoded-to-pass monitors EXIST for self-contradicted geometric claims (the API was available enough to write monitor code that posts a hardcoded YES — that is active-avoidance, not unavailability). The closing sentence currently shoulders argumentative weight that the surrounding paragraphs already carry; ceding that ground in the sentence in question concentrates the active-avoidance claim where the evidence is unambiguous.

patches.find_text: The gaps are systematic: the dome's monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.
patches.replace_text: The gaps are systematic: monitoring is concentrated in domains with publicly-available data feeds — geomagnetic indices, seismic catalogs, eclipse circumstances — while the geometric and theological claims, which have no natural data feed to plug into a validator, fall into the unmonitored bucket. The active-avoidance reading is supplied by the orthogonal evidence above and below: the relabels_standard=true pattern (active editorial rebadging, not an API gap) and, for the Self-Contradicted cohort, the hardcoded-to-pass monitoring described in the next paragraph (validation code exists but is built to return YES — a clearer signal than absence of code).
patches.rationale: Replaces causal-attribution prose ('avoids exactly') with structural framing. Defers the active-avoidance claim to the relabelling pattern in the same paragraph and the SC-hardcoded-to-pass pattern in the next paragraph, both of which carry that argumentative weight more cleanly than a verdict-stratified count can. Acknowledges the kernel of truth in the dome-defender response (publicly-available data feeds) without retreating from the overall argument.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new closing sentence is longer than the original — Part 2b is already prose-heavy.
anticipated_objections.response: The replacement adds ~46 words to a ~6000-word section. The added length is load-bearing: it points the reader at the two specific patterns (relabelling-axis above; hardcoded-monitoring below) that actually carry the active-avoidance argument. A shorter sentence that just says 'the gaps are structural' would lose the explicit hand-off to the surrounding evidence and risk reading like a retreat from the original claim.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new sentence cedes ground on intent — a reader may take away that the dome is doing nothing wrong, just that it lacks APIs.
anticipated_objections.response: The replacement explicitly says the active-avoidance reading is supplied by the orthogonal evidence; it does not cede the reading, it relocates the load-bearing argument. The two patterns it points at (active editorial relabelling; hardcoded-pass monitoring) are stronger active-avoidance signals than the stratification count, so an attacker reading the section gets a sharper attack surface for free.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not delete the closing sentence entirely?
anticipated_objections.response: Deletion would leave the paragraph ending on 'the prediction is a rebadge of an existing answer' which is the relabelling-axis punchline, not a stratification-paragraph wrap-up. The closing sentence's job is to connect the stratification count to the broader argument about non-tests; a structural-framing closer does that job without overclaiming.

anticipated_objections.objection: The find-replace text appears verbatim only once in data/sections.json — confirm before applying.
anticipated_objections.response: Verified at commit head: the string 'The gaps are systematic: the dome's monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging.' occurs exactly once in data/sections.json (in part2b.html, end of relabelling-axis paragraph). No collisions with similar phrasing elsewhere. Patch is additive_safe=false, replaces_exact_string=true, occurrence_count_pre_patch=1.

decider_action_recommended: Apply patch P1 to data/sections.json (single find-replace, one occurrence). Run tests/run.sh (or full build) to confirm no regression in part2b rendering. Close ISS-2318 with resolution 'softened §2b.1 relabelling-paragraph closing sentence to structural framing per EXP-490; active-avoidance reading deferred to relabels_standard=true pattern (same paragraph) and SC hardcoded-to-pass pattern (next paragraph)'.
summary_for_decider: ISS-2318 (moderate, curmudgeon hole 7) on SEC-part2b-stratification-paragraph.c1: the closing sentence 'the dome's monitoring avoids exactly the categories where testing would be most damaging' attributes intent to a pattern that is also consistent with passive API-unavailability (dome defender: 'we monitor what has APIs'). The active-avoidance reading is genuinely supplied by the surrounding paragraphs — the relabelling-axis paragraph (active editorial choice) and the SC-hardcoded-to-pass paragraph (validation built to pass) — but the stratification count alone does not establish it. EXP-490 patches the closing sentence to structural framing that defers the avoidance claim to those orthogonal sources, acknowledging the kernel of truth (publicly-available data feeds) without retreating from the overall argument. One find-replace patch, one occurrence, no rebuild logic touched. Closes hole 7. Holes 1-5 are c1 build-side work covered separately (EXP-441 follow-ups); hole 6 was addressed in earlier analyst runs.
